_comment: EXAMPLE topology for a separate service-host VM that a HEADING OS instance MANAGES from its workspace. The engine ships this generic template; the real values live as private DATA at <data-root>/config/service-host.json (resolved via get_data_config_dir()). The VM is its own entity (its deployment lives outside the engine); the workspace only runs laptop-side management/client scripts against it. Put the VM host ADDRESS in .env as SERVICE_VM_HOST (never here). Post engine/data split: daemon state lives under TWO VM roots — the engine clone (vm_engine_root) and the data overlay (vm_data_root) — so each state_dir names which root it sits under (engine|data). Replace the placeholders below with your own VM's layout.
